sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: Πρῶτον μὲν εὐχῇ τῇδε πρεσβεύω θεῶν
sections.translation: First , in this prayer of mine , I give the place of highest honor among the gods to the first prophet , Earth ; and after her to Themis , for she was the second to take this oracular seat of her mother , as legend tells .

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original: λάχει , θελούσης , οὐδὲ πρὸς βίαν τινός ,
sections.translation: And in the third allotment , with Themis ’ consent and not by force , another Titan , child of Earth , Phoebe , took her seat here . She gave it as a birthday gift to Phoebus , who has his name from Phoebe . Leaving the lake [*] and ridge of Delos ,

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: κέλσας ἐπ ἀκτὰς ναυπόρους τὰς Παλλάδος ,
sections.translation: he landed on Pallas ’ ship-frequented shores , and came to this region and the dwelling places on Parnassus . The children of Hephaistos , [*] road-builders taming the wildness of the untamed land , escorted him with mighty reverence .

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: μολόντα δ αὐτὸν κάρτα τιμαλφεῖ λεώς ,
sections.translation: And at his arrival , the people and Delphus , helmsman and lord of this land , made a great celebration for him . Zeus inspired his heart with prophetic skill and established him as the fourth prophet on this throne ; but Loxias is the spokesman of Zeus , his father .

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: τούτους ἐν εὐχαῖς φροιμιάζομαι θεούς .
sections.translation: These are the gods I place in the beginning of my prayer . And Pallas who stands before the temple [*] is honored in my words ; and I worship the Nymphs where the Corycian [*] rock is hollow , the delight of birds and haunt of gods . Bromius has held the region — I do not forget him —

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original: ἐξ οὗτε Βάκχαις ἐστρατήγησεν θεός ,
sections.translation: ever since he , as a god , led the Bacchantes in war , and contrived for Pentheus death as of a hunted hare . I call on the streams of Pleistus and the strength of Poseidon , and highest Zeus , the Fulfiller ; and then I take my seat as prophetess upon my throne .

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: καὶ νῦν τυχεῖν με τῶν πρὶν εἰσόδων μακρῷ
sections.translation: And may they allow me now to have the best fortune , far better than on my previous entrances . And if there are any from among the Hellenes here , let them enter , in turn , by lot , as is the custom . For I prophesy as the god leads . She enters the temple and after a brief interval returns terror-stricken

sections.label: Section 34
sections.original: ἦ δεινὰ λέξαι , δεινὰ δ ὀφθαλμοῖς δρακεῖν ,
sections.translation: Horrors to tell , horrors for my eyes to see , have sent me back from the house of Loxias ,

sections.label: Section 35
sections.original: πάλιν μ ἔπεμψεν ἐκ δόμων τῶν Λοξίου ,
sections.translation:
so that I have no strength and I cannot walk upright . I am running on hands and knees , with no quickness in my limbs ; for an old woman , overcome with fright , is nothing , or rather she is like a child .
I was on my way to the inner shrine , decked with wreaths ;

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original: ὁρῶ δ ἐπ ὀμφαλῷ μὲν ἄνδρα θεομυσῆ [*]
sections.translation: I saw on the center-stone [*] a man defiled in the eyes of the gods , occupying the seat of suppliants . His hands were dripping blood ; he held a sword just drawn and an olive-branch , from the top of the tree , decorously crowned with a large tuft of wool ,

sections.label: Section 45
sections.original: ἀργῆτι μαλλῷ : τῇδε γὰρ τρανῶς ἐρῶ .
sections.translation:
a shining fleece ; for as to this I can speak clearly .
Before this man an extraordinary band of women slept , seated on thrones . No ! Not women , but rather Gorgons I call them ; and yet I cannot compare them to forms of Gorgons either .

sections.label: Section 50
sections.original: εἶδόν ποτ ἤδη Φινέως γεγραμμένας
sections.translation: Once before I saw some creatures in a painting , [*] carrying off the feast of Phineus ; but these are wingless in appearance , black , altogether disgusting ; they snore with repulsive breaths , they drip from their eyes hateful drops ;

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: καὶ κόσμος οὔτε πρὸς θεῶν ἀγάλματα
sections.translation: their attire is not fit to bring either before the statues of the gods or into the homes of men . I have never seen the tribe that produced this company , nor the land that boasts of rearing this brood with impunity and does not grieve for its labor afterwards .

sections.label: Section 60
sections.original: τἀντεῦθεν ἤδη τῶνδε δεσπότῃ δόμων
sections.translation: Let what is to come now be the concern of the master of this house , powerful Loxias himself . He is a prophet of healing , a reader of portents , and for others a purifier of homes . Exit

sections.label: Section 64
sections.original: οὔτοι προδώσω : διὰ τέλους δέ σοι φύλαξ
sections.translation: No ! I will not abandon you . Your guardian to the end ,

sections.label: Section 65
sections.original: ἐγγὺς παρεστὼς καὶ πρόσω δ ἀποστατῶν
sections.translation: close by your side or far removed , I will not be gentle to your enemies . So now you see these mad women overcome ; these loathsome maidens have fallen asleep , old women , ancient children , with whom

sections.label: Section 70
sections.original: θεῶν τις οὐδ ἄνθρωπος οὐδὲ θήρ ποτε :
sections.translation: no god or man or beast ever mingles . They were even born for evil , since they live in evil gloom and in Tartarus under the earth , creatures hateful to men and to the Olympian gods . Nevertheless , escape and do not be cowardly .

sections.label: Section 75
sections.original: ἐλῶσι γάρ σε καὶ δι ἠπείρου μακρᾶς
sections.translation: For as you go always over the earth that wanderers tread , they will drive you on , even across the wide mainland , beyond the sea and the island cities . Do not grow weary too soon , brooding on this labor , but when you have come to Pallas ’ city ,

sections.label: Section 80
sections.original: ἵζου παλαιὸν ἄγκαθεν λαβὼν βρέτας .
sections.translation: sit down and hold in your arms her ancient image . And there , with judges of your case and speeches of persuasive charm , we shall find means to release you completely from your labors . For I persuaded you to take your mother ’ s life .

sections.label: Section 85
sections.original: ἄναξ Ἄπολλον , οἶσθα μὲν τὸ μὴ δικεῖν :
sections.translation: Lord Apollo , you know how to do no wrong ; and , since you know this , learn not to be neglectful also . For your power to do good is assured .

sections.label: Section 88
sections.original: μέμνησο , μὴ φόβος σὲ νικάτω φρένας .
sections.translation: Remember , do not let fear overpower your heart . You , Hermes , my blood brother , born of the same father ,

sections.label: Section 90
sections.original: Ἑρμῆ , φύλασσε : κάρτα δ ὢν ἐπώνυμος
sections.translation: watch over him ; true to your name , be his guide , [*] shepherding this suppliant of mine — truly Zeus respects this right of outlaws — as he is sped on towards mortals with the fortune of a good escort . Exit . Orestes departs escorted by Hermes . The Ghost of Clytaemestra appears .

sections.label: Section 94
sections.original: εὕδοιτ ἄν , ὠή , καὶ καθευδουσῶν τί δεῖ ;
sections.translation: Sleep on ! Aha ! Yet what need is there of sleepers ?

sections.label: Section 95
sections.original: ἐγὼ δ ὑφ ὑμῶν ὧδ ἀπητιμασμένη
sections.translation: It is due to you that I am thus dishonored among the other dead ; because of those I killed the dead never cease to reproach me , and I wander in disgrace . I tell you that I am most greatly accused by them .

sections.label: Section 100
sections.original: παθοῦσα δ οὕτω δεινὰ πρὸς τῶν φιλτάτων ,
sections.translation: And yet , although I have suffered cruelly in this way from my nearest kin , no divine power is angry on my behalf , slaughtered as I have been by the hands of a matricide . See these gashes in my heart , and from where they came ! For the sleeping mind has clear vision ,

sections.label: Section 105
sections.original: ἐν ἡμέρᾳ δὲ μοῖρ ἀπρόσκοπος [*] βροτῶν .
sections.translation:
but in the daytime the fate of mortals is unforeseeable .
Truly , you have lapped up many of my offerings — wineless libations , a sober appeasement ; and I have sacrificed banquets in the solemn night upon a hearth of fire at an hour unshared by any god .

sections.label: Section 110
sections.original: καὶ πάντα ταῦτα λὰξ ὁρῶ πατούμενα .
sections.translation: I see all this trampled under foot . But he has escaped and is gone , like a fawn ; lightly indeed , from the middle of snares , he has rushed away mocking at you . Hear me , since I plead for my life ,

sections.label: Section 115
sections.original: ψυχῆς , φρονήσατ , ὦ κατὰ χθονὸς θεαί .
sections.translation: awake to consciousness , goddesses of the underworld ! For in a dream I , Clytaemestra , now invoke you .

sections.label: Section 117
sections.original: ʽμ̔υγμός . ʼ
sections.translation: ( whine ) The Chorus begins to move uneasily , uttering a whining sound .

sections.label: Section 118
sections.original: μύζοιτ ἄν , ἁνὴρ δ οἴχεται φεύγων πρόσω :
sections.translation: Whine , if you will ! But the man is gone , fled far away . For he has friends that are not like mine !

sections.label: Section 120
sections.original: ʽμ̔υγμός . ʼ
sections.translation: ( whine ) The Chorus continues to whine .

sections.label: Section 121
sections.original: ἄγαν ὑπνώσσεις κοὐ κατοικτίζεις πάθος :
sections.translation: You are too drowsy and do not pity my suffering . Orestes , the murderer of me , his mother , is gone !

sections.label: Section 123
sections.original: ʽὡ̓γμός . ʼ [*]
sections.translation: ( moan ) The Chorus begins to moan

sections.label: Section 124
sections.original: ὤζεις , ὑπνώσσεις : οὐκ ἀναστήσει τάχος ;
sections.translation: You moan , you drowse — will you not get up at once ? Is it your destiny to do anything other than cause harm ?

sections.label: Section 126
sections.original: ʽὡ̓γμός . ʼ
sections.translation: ( moan ) The Chorus continues to moan .

sections.label: Section 127
sections.original: ὕπνος πόνος τε κύριοι συνωμόται
sections.translation: Sleep and toil , effective conspirators , have destroyed the force of the dreadful dragoness .

sections.label: Section 129
sections.original: ʽμ̔υγμὸς διπλοῦς ὀξύς . ʼ
sections.translation: Catch him ! Catch him ! Catch him ! Catch him ! Look sharp !

sections.label: Section 131
sections.original: ὄναρ διώκεις θῆρα , κλαγγαίνεις δ ἅπερ
sections.translation: In a dream you are hunting your prey , and are barking like a dog that never leaves off its keenness for the work . What are you doing ? Get up ; do not let fatigue overpower you , and do not ignore my misery because you have been softened by sleep .

sections.label: Section 135
sections.original: ἄλγησον ἧπαρ ἐνδίκοις ὀνείδεσιν : [*]
sections.translation: Sting your heart with merited reproaches ; for reproach becomes a spur to the right-minded . Send after him a gust of bloody breath , shrivel him with the vapor , the fire from your guts , follow him , wither him with fresh pursuit ! The Ghost of Clytaemestra disappears ; the Furies , roused by their leader , awake one after the other .

sections.label: Section 140
sections.original: ἔγειρ , ἔγειρε καὶ σὺ τήνδ , ἐγὼ δὲ σέ .
sections.translation: Awake ! Wake her up , as I wake you . Still asleep ? Get up , shake off sleep , let us see if any part of this beginning [*] is in vain .

sections.label: Section 1.143
sections.original: ἰοὺ ἰοὺ πόπαξ . ἐπάθομεν , φίλαι ,—
sections.translation:
Oh , oh ! Alas ! We have suffered , friends .
Indeed I have suffered much and all in vain .

sections.label: Section 1.145
sections.original: ἐπάθομεν πάθος δυσαχές , ὦ πόποι ,
sections.translation:
We have suffered very painfully , oh ! an unbearable evil . The beast has escaped from our nets and is gone .
Overcome by sleep , I have lost my prey .

sections.label: Section 1.149
sections.original: ἰὼ παῖ Διός , ἐπίκλοπος πέλει ,—
sections.translation: Oh ! Child of Zeus , you have become a thief —

sections.label: Section 1.150
sections.original: νέος δὲ γραίας δαίμονας καθιππάσω ,—
sections.translation:
you , a youth , have ridden down old divinities — by showing respect to your suppliant , a godless man and cruel to a parent ; although you are a god , you have stolen away a man that killed his mother .
What is there here that anyone shall call just ?

sections.label: Section 2.155
sections.original: ἐμοὶ δ ὄνειδος ἐξ ὀνειράτων μολὸν
sections.translation: Reproach , coming to me in a dream , struck me like a charioteer with goad held tight , under my heart , under my vitals .

sections.label: Section 2.160
sections.original: πάρεστι μαστίκτορος δαΐου δαμίου
sections.translation: I can feel the cruel , the very cruel chill of the executioner ’ s destroying scourge .

sections.label: Section 2.162
sections.original: τοιαῦτα δρῶσιν οἱ νεώτεροι θεοί ,
sections.translation: They do such things , the younger gods , who rule , wholly beyond justice , a throne dripping blood ,

sections.label: Section 2.165
sections.original: περὶ πόδα , περὶ κάρα .—
sections.translation:
about its foot , about its head .
I can see the center-stone of the earth defiled with a terrible pollution of blood .

sections.label: Section 3.170
sections.original: μυχὸν [*] ἔχρανας [*] αὐτόσσυτος , αὐτόκλητος ,
sections.translation: with pollution at its hearth , at his own urging , at his own bidding ; against the law of the gods , he has honored mortal things and caused the ancient allotments to decay .

sections.label: Section 3.175
sections.original: ὑπό τε γᾶν φυγὼν [*] οὔ ποτ ἐλευθεροῦται .
sections.translation: even if he escapes beneath the earth , he is never set free . A suppliant , he will acquire another avenger from his family . [*]

sections.label: Section 178
sections.original: ἔξω , κελεύω , τῶνδε δωμάτων τάχος
sections.translation: Out , I order you ! Go away from this house at once ,

sections.label: Section 180
sections.original: χωρεῖτ , ἀπαλλάσσεσθε μαντικῶν μυχῶν ,
sections.translation: leave my prophetic sanctuary , so that you may not be struck by a winged glistening snake [*] shot forth from a golden bow-string , and painfully release black foam , vomiting the clots of blood you have drained from mortals .

sections.label: Section 185
sections.original: οὔτοι δόμοισι τοῖσδε χρίμπτεσθαι πρέπει :
sections.translation: It is not right for you to approach this house ; no , your place is where the punishments are beheading , gouging out of eyes , cutting of throats , and where young men ’ s virility is ruined by destruction of seed ; where there is mutilation and stoning , and where those who are impaled beneath their spine moan long and piteously .

sections.label: Section 190
sections.original: ὑπὸ ῥάχιν [*] παγέντες . ἆρ ἀκούετε
sections.translation: Do you hear what sort of feast is your delight ? You are detested by the gods for it . The whole fashion of your form sets it forth . Creatures like you should live in the den of a blood-drinking lion , and not inflict pollution

sections.label: Section 195
sections.original: ἐν τοῖσδε Πυθίοισι [*] τρίβεσθαι μύσος .
sections.translation: on all near you in this oracular shrine . Be gone , you goats without a herdsman ! No god loves such a flock .

sections.label: Section 198
sections.original: ἄναξ Ἄπολλον , ἀντάκουσον ἐν μέρει .
sections.translation: Lord Apollo , hear our reply in turn . You yourself are not partially guilty of this deed ;

sections.label: Section 200
sections.original: ἀλλ εἷς [*] τὸ πᾶν ἔπραξας ὢν [*] παναίτιος .
sections.translation: you alone have done it all , and are wholly guilty .

sections.label: Section 201
sections.original: πῶς δή ; τοσοῦτο μῆκος ἔκτεινον λόγου .
sections.translation: What do you mean ? Draw out the length of your speech this much .

sections.label: Section 202
sections.original: ἔχρησας ὥστε τὸν ξένον μητροκτονεῖν .
sections.translation: Through your oracle , you directed the stranger to kill his mother .

sections.label: Section 203
sections.original: ἔχρησα ποινὰς τοῦ πατρὸς [*] πρᾶξαι . [*] τί [*] μήν ;
sections.translation: Through my oracle , I directed him to exact vengeance for his father . What of it ?
